description: Entity types representing AWS governance, compliance, and operations resources including CloudFormation, Service Catalog, Backup, License Manager, Resource Groups, and Tag Editor.

entityTypes.description: An AWS CloudFormation stack — a collection of AWS resources provisioned and managed as a single unit from a template.
An AWS CloudFormation StackSet that enables creating, updating, or deleting stacks across multiple accounts and regions with a single operation.
A summary of proposed changes to an AWS CloudFormation stack, allowing review before execution.
An AWS Service Catalog portfolio — a collection of products (CloudFormation templates) with access control and configuration.
An AWS Service Catalog product — a CloudFormation template with versioning and metadata that can be shared across portfolios.
An instance of an AWS Service Catalog product that has been provisioned into an account (a running CloudFormation stack).
An AWS Backup vault — a container for recovery points (backups) that is encrypted and access-controlled.
An AWS Backup plan — a policy that defines when and how AWS resources are backed up, including schedules, retention, and lifecycle rules.
An AWS Backup selection — a mapping of AWS resources (by ARN, tag, or resource type) to a backup plan.
An AWS Backup recovery point (backup) stored in a backup vault, representing a point-in-time snapshot of a resource.
An AWS License Manager license configuration — a set of rules encoding the terms of a software license (e.g., per-core or per-instance limits).
An AWS Resource Groups group — a logical container for AWS resources defined by a tag-based or CloudFormation stack-based query.
A tagged AWS resource discovered via the Resource Groups Tagging API, providing a cross-service view of all resources and their tags in a region.
